Home/Qarbala palms for farms and gardens.

Regional palm selection

Qarbala palms for farms and gardens.

Qarbala is a distinctive regional date palm variety carried by the nursery alongside Aseel, giving buyers a second authentic option when planning a farm, garden or landscape project. Qarbala palms are listed from approximately 1 ft to 10 ft, covering young nursery stock through to more established plants. Current availability, exact size and condition are always confirmed directly with the nursery team before a final quotation is issued, since stock changes with the season.

Enquire about Qarbala →
Height rangeApproximately 1–10 ft
AvailabilityConfirmed before quotation
Use casesFarm and garden enquiries
SupportDelivery and installation assessment

What buyers need to know

01

What makes Qarbala different

Qarbala carries its own regional character and is sought after by buyers who want variety within a planting, or who specifically know the name from local growing communities. It is commonly chosen alongside Aseel where a project wants a mix of varieties rather than a single-type orchard or garden.

  • Regional authenticity
  • Complements Aseel plantings
  • Recognised local variety
02

Choose by outcome

The right size depends on the objective. Smaller plants (1–3 ft) suit buyers who want young palms to establish over time at a lower initial cost. Mid-range plants (4–7 ft) are a common choice for farmhouse gardens wanting a visible plant sooner. Larger Qarbala stock (8–10 ft) suits farms and landscape projects wanting a more immediate visual result.

  • Young planting stock
  • Established garden palms
  • Project quantities
03

Plan delivery early

Larger palms need appropriate vehicle access at both ends of the journey, safe unloading space, and a planting location prepared in advance so the plant is not left waiting in transit conditions longer than necessary.

  • Destination review
  • Access assessment
  • Installation scope
04

Get a useful quotation

Send your city, required quantity, preferred height and intended use (farm, garden, commercial project) in the first WhatsApp message. This single step reduces back-and-forth and avoids inaccurate first estimates.

  • City
  • Quantity and height
  • Purpose and timeline

Direct, structured enquiry

Send the right details.
Get a more useful answer.
